steps.title: Find the radius using the distance from center C to point A
steps.content:
The radius is the distance from $C(h, k)$ to $A(h+1, k+\sqrt{230})$:
$$r = \sqrt{(h+1-h)^2 + (k+\sqrt{230}-k)^2} = \sqrt{1^2 + (\sqrt{230})^2} = \sqrt{1 + 230} = \sqrt{231}$$

steps.title: Apply the right angle condition with the Pythagorean theorem
steps.content:
Triangle $ACB$ has a right angle at $C$ (given: $\angle ACB = 90°$). Both $CA$ and $CB$ are radii, so $CA = CB = \sqrt{231}$. Since the angle between them is $90°$, apply the Pythagorean theorem directly:
$$AB^2 = CA^2 + CB^2 = (\sqrt{231})^2 + (\sqrt{231})^2 = 231 + 231 = 462$$
$$AB = \sqrt{462}$$
That matches choice D — D is correct.
